    - "User can configure Bedrock region (default us-east-1) and a Bedrock model ID via free-text inputs"
    - "User can pick one of 4 Bedrock auth methods: default credential chain, explicit access keys, SSO profile, Bedrock API key"
    - "Switching auth method swaps which secret fields are visible WITHOUT clearing previously-entered secrets in other modes (Pitfall 10 — preserve invariant)"
    - "Test connection probe issues a 1-token chat completion and returns ok:true on success / ok:false with truncated 200-char errorMessage on failure (probeBedrock NEVER throws)"
    - "AIClient.invokeStream + invoke (the existing seams from Plan 07-05) automatically dispatch to Bedrock via resolveAdapter('bedrock', ...) — no caller-side change"
    - "Disclosure modal substitutes user-configured region into the Bedrock base URL (https://bedrock-runtime.{region}.amazonaws.com)"
    - "Logger redacts bedrockApiKey, accessKeyId, secretAccessKey, sessionToken in messages and headers"
    - "data.json bedrock config is shape-guarded on load; malformed input collapses to safe defaults"
    - "README ## Network usage section enumerates the AWS Bedrock regional endpoint format alongside the existing 4 AI provider hosts + leetcode.com"
    - "Production bundle stays under 1.2 MB ceiling after @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock add (measured baseline ~996 KB, expected ~1090 KB, headroom ~109 KB)"

---

<objective>
Add AWS Bedrock as a 5th first-class AI provider so users can run AI Debug / AI Review against Bedrock-hosted models without a LiteLLM/Bedrock-Access-Gateway proxy. Forward-ports the AIPROV-FUT-01 deferral via `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.107` (which bundles `aws4fetch` for SigV4 internally), 4-option auth dropdown (default-chain / explicit-keys / SSO-profile / Bedrock-API-key), and an in-plugin `~/.aws/credentials` INI parser (because `@aws-sdk/credential-providers` does NOT bundle in esbuild — measured this research session, fails with 9 unresolved Node imports).

Plan inherits the new transport tier from Plan 08.1-01 — Bedrock CORS-rejects native fetch (AWS does not send CORS-permissive headers from `bedrock-runtime.*.amazonaws.com`), so Bedrock will exercise TIER 2 → TIER 3 buffered, which is the working state Phase 08 dogfood validated.

Purpose: Plugin gains native Bedrock without a proxy layer; reduces friction for users on AWS-native infrastructure; respects the locked decisions A–E from CONTEXT.md verbatim.

Output: New `bedrock.ts` adapter mirroring `anthropic.ts`; new `awsCredentials.ts` Node-fs reader; new `displayBaseUrl.ts` helper; modifications to 7 existing files (providers/index.ts, types.ts, disclosure.ts, SettingsTab.ts, SettingsStore.ts, logger.ts, package.json, README.md); 7 new test files + 1 extension to the existing readme-network-use test; bundle measurement gate.
</objective>

  - export async function probeAnthropic(cfg, fetcher): Promise<ProbeResult>;  // never throws

From @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.107 (npm — to install in Task 1):
  - export function createAmazonBedrock(opts: {
      region?: string;
      accessKeyId?: string;
      secretAccessKey?: string;
      sessionToken?: string;
      apiKey?: string;       // Bedrock API key bearer; SDK falls back to AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK env
      headers?: Record<string, string>;
      fetch?: FetchFn;
    }): (modelId: string) => LanguageModelV1;

  - Internal SigV4 via aws4fetch@^1.0.20; eventstream decode via @smithy/eventstream-codec@^4.0.1
  - DOES NOT pull @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime

  <action>
    PHASE A — install dependency. `npm install @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.107` (verified 2026-05-16 in RESEARCH; peer-deps match our pinned `@ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.78`). Confirm package-lock.json updated; transitive aws4fetch + @smithy/eventstream-codec resolve. DO NOT install `@aws-sdk/credential-providers` — RESEARCH §Pitfall 5 confirms it fails esbuild with 9 unresolved Node imports.

    PHASE B — type widening. In `src/ai/types.ts`:
    1. Widen `AIProvider` union: `'anthropic' | 'openai' | 'openrouter' | 'ollama' | 'custom' | 'bedrock'`.
    2. Extend `prettyName` switch with `case 'bedrock': return 'AWS Bedrock';` — TypeScript exhaustiveness check now surfaces every other call site that needs updating (S4 shared pattern).
    3. Add `export interface BedrockProviderConfig extends ProviderConfig { region: string; modelId: string; authMethod: 'default-chain' | 'access-keys' | 'sso-profile' | 'api-key'; accessKeyId?: string; secretAccessKey?: string; ssoProfile?: string; bedrockApiKey?: string; }` per 08.1-PATTERNS.md "Plan 08.1-02 #src/ai/types.ts" code block.

    PHASE C — credentials parser. Create `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts` mirroring 08.1-PATTERNS.md "Plan 08.1-02 #src/ai/awsCredentials.ts" full code block:
    - `nodeRequire<T>` shim copied byte-for-byte from `src/auth/BrowserWindowLogin.ts:12-22` (S2 shared pattern).
    - `resolveAwsCredentials({ source: 'env-or-default-profile' } | { source: 'profile'; profile: string })` — env vars first; then `nodeRequire<typeof import('fs')>('fs')` + `path` + `os`; resolves `~/.aws/credentials`; passes to `parseIniProfile`.
    - `parseIniProfile(text, profile)` — handles bare-name section AND `[profile <name>]` syntax; ignores `#` and `;` comments and blank lines; reads `aws_access_key_id`, `aws_secret_access_key`, `aws_session_token`.

    PHASE D — display URL helper. Create `src/ai/displayBaseUrl.ts` per 08.1-PATTERNS.md "Plan 08.1-02 #src/ai/displayBaseUrl.ts" code block: `getDisplayBaseUrl(provider, cfg)` — exhaustive switch; for Bedrock, returns `\`https://bedrock-runtime.${cfg.region || 'us-east-1'}.amazonaws.com\``; for others, returns `cfg.baseUrl`.

    PHASE E — SettingsStore extension. In `src/settings/SettingsStore.ts`:
    1. Add `bedrock` entry to `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_CONFIGS` map per 08.1-PATTERNS.md code block (region: 'us-east-1', modelId: 'anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0', authMethod: 'default-chain', all 4 secrets ''). Type the map as `Record<AIProvider, ProviderConfig | BedrockProviderConfig>`.
    2. Add `'bedrock'` to `VALID_AI_PROVIDERS` set.
    3. Add `function sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig(raw, defaults): BedrockProviderConfig` next to existing sanitizeProviderConfig — per 08.1-PATTERNS.md code block; uses `VALID_AUTH_METHODS = new Set(['default-chain', 'access-keys', 'sso-profile', 'api-key'])`; falls back to defaults on unknown authMethod; PRESERVES all 4 secret fields verbatim regardless of authMethod (Pitfall 10).
    4. In the `load()` providerConfigs hydration block, add a `bedrock: sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig(...)` entry per 08.1-PATTERNS.md code block.

    PHASE F — logger redaction. In `src/shared/logger.ts`:
    1. Extend the `REDACT` regex at line 17 with the new alternates per 08.1-PATTERNS.md code block: `accesskeyid|secretaccesskey|aws_session_token|bedrockapikey|sso_?profile`.
    2. Extend the `SECRET_VALUE_PATTERN` regex at line 69 second alternation per the same code block: `aws_access_key_id|aws_secret_access_key|aws_session_token|aws_bearer_token_bedrock|accesskeyid|secretaccesskey|bedrockapikey`.
    Use the BEARER_VALUE_PATTERN-then-SECRET_VALUE_PATTERN order (Plan 07-01 D-04 invariant — Authorization: Bearer redacts at both layers).

    PHASE G — write all unit tests for the foundation files. Create `tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts`, `tests/settings/SettingsStore.bedrock.test.ts`, `tests/shared/logger.bedrock.test.ts` per the `<behavior>` block. Use the `vi.stubGlobal('activeWindow', { require: fakeRequire })` pattern from 08.1-PATTERNS.md for `awsCredentials.test.ts` (mirroring `tests/ai/obsidianFetch.test.ts` lines 100-119). Use the existing test patterns in `tests/settings/SettingsStore.test.ts` and `tests/shared/logger.test.ts` for the other two.

    Implementation per AIPROV-02 (extends API-key storage policy to Bedrock secrets with logger redaction; per D-13 / D-14 from v1.0 — plain-text local-only storage with explicit README disclosure inheriting Plan 07-06 documentation), AIPROV-FUT-01 (forward-port via @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock per CONTEXT decision C).
  </action>

Returns `provider(cfg.modelId || 'anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0')`.

    - `streamBedrock(cfg, fetcher, prompt, signal)` — sync return of streamText result.
    - `invokeBedrockBuffered(cfg, fetcher, prompt, signal)` — async generateText with usage extraction.

    Implementation per AIPROV-FUT-01 forward-port (creates the actual provider) and AIPROV-04 (disclosure modal handles Bedrock via existing per-provider gate inherited from Plan 07-05's AIClient seam — no caller-side change needed; resolveAdapter's `case 'bedrock'` slots into the existing dispatcher).

Insert `.addOption('bedrock', 'AWS Bedrock')` so the locked dropdown order from 07-UI-SPEC is preserved (Bedrock joins as the 6th option after `custom` to follow the alphabetical-by-add-time precedent — RESEARCH.md does not require a specific position; planner choice). If `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` flags 'AWS Bedrock' as not-sentence-case, add the brand to the allowlist in `eslint.config.mts` mirroring the Plan 07-03 precedent for 'OpenAI'/'OpenRouter'.

    2. Locate `renderAIProviderForm`'s switch (around line 316). Add a new `case 'bedrock':` branch implementing the 4-option auth dropdown UI per 08.1-PATTERNS.md "Plan 08.1-02 #src/settings/SettingsTab.ts" full code block:
       - Cast cfg to BedrockProviderConfig: `const bcfg = cfg as BedrockProviderConfig;`
       - Region row: `new Setting(containerEl).setName('Region').setDesc('AWS region for Bedrock runtime endpoint. Default: us-east-1.').addText(...)` — onChange calls `setProviderConfig(active, { ...current, region: v })`.
       - Model ID row: same shape, label `'Model ID'`, description per 08.1-PATTERNS.md.
       - Auth method dropdown: `.addDropdown(d => d.addOption('default-chain', 'Default credential chain (recommended)').addOption('access-keys', 'Explicit access keys').addOption('sso-profile', 'SSO profile').addOption('api-key', 'Bedrock API key').setValue(bcfg.authMethod).onChange(...))`. The onChange MUST call `this.display()` after setProviderConfig so the conditional fields swap atomically (Phase 06 PREVIEW-02 + Phase 07 active-provider precedent).
       - Conditional secret rows per `bcfg.authMethod` per the auth-mode UI matrix (Pattern 5 in RESEARCH.md):
         * `default-chain` → no secret rows; helper-text Setting describes "Plugin uses AWS credentials from your environment or `~/.aws/credentials` `[default]` profile."
         * `access-keys` → 2 rows: Access key ID + Secret access key, both `<input type="password">`.
         * `sso-profile` → 1 row: Profile name (text); helper text mentions running `aws sso login --profile <name>`.
         * `api-key` → 1 row: Bedrock API key, `<input type="password">`; helper text mentions IAM > Users > Service-specific credentials.
       - Test connection button: present per existing Settings precedent; calls `this.plugin.testActiveAIConnection()` (existing from Plan 07-04).
       - **PITFALL 10 INVARIANT (CRITICAL):** The auth-method dropdown's onChange must NEVER clear other secret fields. Setting `authMethod: 'api-key'` ONLY changes which fields RENDER, never which are STORED. All 4 secret fields (`accessKeyId`, `secretAccessKey`, `ssoProfile`, `bedrockApiKey`) live in the config simultaneously; only the active one is consumed at probe/invoke time. The `sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig` shape-guard from Task 1 already preserves all 4 regardless of authMethod.

    3. If `renderAIProviderForm` switch has a `modelPlaceholder` companion or any per-provider helper, also extend it with `case 'bedrock'` to keep the exhaustive switch (S4 shared pattern). For Bedrock, the standard "Model" row is replaced by "Model ID" inside the case block, so the generic Model row from the outer scope MUST be hidden when `active === 'bedrock'` (planner choice: either guard the generic Model row with `if (active !== 'bedrock')` mirroring how `'ollama'` skips the API key row, OR omit it entirely from the switch case). Choose the form that minimizes diff against existing code.

    PHASE B — README extension. In `README.md`, locate the `## Network usage` section established by Plan 07-06. Add Bedrock as the 5th AI provider per 08.1-PATTERNS.md "Plan 08.1-02 #README.md" code block:

    PHASE D — Final bundle gate. Run `npm run build && npm run check:bundle-size`. Expected output: bundle ~1090 KB, ceiling 1.2 MB, exit 0. If bundle is over 1.2 MB:

    Per RESEARCH.md measurement, this branch is unlikely — expected ~1090 KB, well under ceiling.

    PHASE E — Final integration smoke. Run `npm run lint && npm test -- --run` (full suite) — all green.

    Implementation per AIPROV-FUT-01 (settings UI delivery surface), AIPROV-02 (masked password inputs for the 3 secret fields per the locked AIPROV-02 plain-text-with-disclosure posture), AIPROV-04 (README ## Network usage extension makes Bedrock disclosure parity with the other 4 providers).

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Plugin renderer → AWS Bedrock regional endpoint | Bedrock calls cross from the renderer to `https://bedrock-runtime.{region}.amazonaws.com`; SigV4 signing happens inside `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock` via vendored aws4fetch — plugin never sees signing bytes. |
| Plugin renderer → user filesystem (~/.aws/credentials) | New attack surface: Plan 08.1-02's awsCredentials.ts reads the user's AWS credentials file via Node fs from the Electron renderer. Reads MUST be tightly bounded — only the requested profile's `aws_access_key_id` / `aws_secret_access_key` / `aws_session_token` fields are returned to callers. |
| User input fields (Settings UI) → data.json (plain-text) | 3 new secret fields (accessKeyId, secretAccessKey, bedrockApiKey) inherit AIPROV-02's plain-text-local-only storage policy with masked input + README disclosure + logger redaction. |
| Bedrock provider host → AI Debug modal | AI provider responses render via MarkdownRenderer.render (no innerHTML); same trust posture as the existing 4 providers. |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-08.1-01 | Information Disclosure | Native fetch transport (inherited from Plan 08.1-01) | mitigate | Inherited via Plan 08.1-01's TIER 1 `credentials: 'omit'` override. Bedrock is unaffected because Bedrock CORS-rejects native fetch and falls to TIER 2/3 (RESEARCH §Pitfall 1 / Assumption A4) — but the inherited safety net stays in place. |
| T-08.1-02 | Information Disclosure | Bedrock secrets (accessKeyId / secretAccessKey / bedrockApiKey / aws_session_token) leaked to logs | mitigate | Logger REDACT regex extension at line 17 + SECRET_VALUE_PATTERN extension at line 69 (Task 1 Phase F). Field-name match (accesskeyid|secretaccesskey|bedrockapikey|aws_session_token|aws_bearer_token_bedrock) blocks both header and message logging. Settings UI uses `<input type="password">` for all 3 user-typed secret fields per AIPROV-02 (Task 3). Sanitize shape-guard in SettingsStore preserves but never exposes the field shape (Task 1 Phase E). Tests in `tests/shared/logger.bedrock.test.ts` (Task 1) assert each pattern matches. |
| T-08.1-03 | Information Disclosure | ~/.aws/credentials filesystem read leaking credential file contents into logs or disclosure modal | mitigate | `src/ai/awsCredentials.ts` (Task 1 Phase C) returns ONLY the requested profile's `aws_access_key_id` + `aws_secret_access_key` + `aws_session_token` fields to the AI SDK constructor — never raw file bytes, never the full INI map. Read failures (ENOENT, permission errors) collapse to empty object; never throw or log raw exception messages. The shim is the same canonical pattern as `src/auth/BrowserWindowLogin.ts:nodeRequire` (Phase 02 precedent). Tests in `tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts` assert: missing file returns {}, malformed file returns {}, named profile is correctly scoped. |
| T-08.1-04 | Information Disclosure | Bedrock disclosure modal showing wrong region in base URL (or showing the entire credentials file path) | mitigate | `src/ai/displayBaseUrl.ts:getDisplayBaseUrl(provider, cfg)` (Task 1 Phase D) substitutes ONLY `cfg.region` into a fixed-shape format string `https://bedrock-runtime.${region}.amazonaws.com`. Default `'us-east-1'` falls in if region is missing. `tests/ai/disclosure.bedrock.test.ts` (Task 2) asserts the substituted region appears in the modal body. |
| T-08.1-mode-switch | Information Disclosure / Tampering | Auth-method dropdown switching clears secrets in inactive modes | mitigate | Pitfall 10 from RESEARCH.md. `sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig` (Task 1) PRESERVES all 4 secret fields verbatim regardless of authMethod. Settings UI (Task 3) only changes which rows RENDER, never which fields are STORED. Tests in `tests/settings/SettingsStore.bedrock.test.ts` (Task 1) assert: switching authMethod from access-keys to api-key and back leaves accessKeyId + secretAccessKey unchanged byte-for-byte. |
| T-08.1-SC | Tampering | Supply-chain — npm install of `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.107` introduces malicious code via dependency confusion | mitigate | Package Legitimacy Audit in 08.1-RESEARCH.md confirmed `[OK]` from slopcheck for `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock` (this research session, 2026-05-16); package is from the Vercel AI SDK monorepo (github.com/vercel/ai); peer-deps match our existing pin (`@ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.78`); transitive aws4fetch + @smithy/eventstream-codec are AWS-official (smithy-lang) or 7-year-stable (mhart/aws4fetch). Pin is exact (`4.0.107`). No `[ASSUMED]` or `[SUS]` packages in this plan — `@aws-sdk/credential-providers` REJECTED for non-supply-chain reasons (does not bundle in esbuild — Pitfall 5). |
| T-08.1-bundle | Denial of Service | Bedrock package bloats the bundle past the 1.2 MB ceiling, forcing an architectural deviation | accept (with measurement gate) | Bundle measurement in Task 3 Phase D enforces the 1.2 MB ceiling before this plan is declared done. RESEARCH measured baseline 996.9 KB → 1090.8 KB after Bedrock add — 109 KB headroom under ceiling. If measurement deviates from this expectation, planner gates with Rule 3 ceiling bump (precedent: Phase 07-03, Phase 08-02). Task 3 acceptance criteria explicitly invoke `npm run check:bundle-size`. |
</threat_model>

<verification>
- `npm test -- --run` (full suite) passes — all foundation, adapter, settings, disclosure, logger, README tests green
- `npm run lint` passes (prelint LC-isolation gates from Plan 08.1-01 still green; no `@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports` violations in awsCredentials.ts)
- `npm run build` succeeds (esbuild produces main.js)
- `npm run check:bundle-size` reports under 1.2 MB ceiling (expected ~1090 KB)
- `npm test -- --run tests/ai/providers/bedrock.test.ts tests/ai/providers/bedrock.auth.test.ts tests/ai/awsCredentials.test.ts` — Bedrock-side green
- `npm test -- --run tests/settings/SettingsStore.bedrock.test.ts tests/shared/logger.bedrock.test.ts tests/ai/disclosure.bedrock.test.ts` — settings/logger/disclosure green
- `grep -c 'bedrock-runtime' README.md` ≥ 1 (network use disclosure complete)
- `grep -c "case 'bedrock'" src/ai/providers/index.ts` → 1; `grep -c "case 'bedrock'" src/settings/SettingsTab.ts` → 1; `grep -c "case 'bedrock'" src/ai/types.ts` → 1; `grep -c "case 'bedrock'" src/ai/displayBaseUrl.ts` → 1 (S4 exhaustive switch parity)
- Manual UAT (per 08.1-VALIDATION.md Manual-Only): real-Obsidian Bedrock connectivity test against default-chain auth + one explicit auth method
</verification>

<success_criteria>
1. User picks AWS Bedrock from the active-AI-provider dropdown; the Settings sub-form renders Region + Model ID + Auth method dropdown + the conditional secret fields per active auth mode.
2. User can configure all 4 auth methods (default-chain / explicit access keys / SSO profile / Bedrock API key); switching modes preserves entered secrets in inactive modes (Pitfall 10).
3. "Test connection" probe issues a 1-token chat completion via `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock` and reports success/failure as a Notice (~$0.0001 cost). probeBedrock NEVER throws.
4. AI Debug palette command + fence-row button work against Bedrock (modal renders the response; transport falls to buffered tier per Pitfall 1 — acceptable per CONTEXT decision A).
5. First-call disclosure modal renders `Active provider: AWS Bedrock — https://bedrock-runtime.{region}.amazonaws.com` with the user-configured region substituted.
6. README's `## Network usage` section enumerates the Bedrock regional endpoint alongside the 4 existing AI providers + leetcode.com.
7. data.json schema gracefully handles malformed Bedrock config blobs via sanitizeBedrockProviderConfig — no crash on downgrade or corruption.
8. Logger redacts `accessKeyId` / `secretAccessKey` / `bedrockApiKey` / `aws_session_token` field names AND value-pattern matches across messages and headers.
9. Bundle stays under 1.2 MB ceiling after `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.107` install (no Rule 3 deviation expected per RESEARCH measurement).
10. AIPROV-FUT-01 forward-port complete — users no longer need a LiteLLM proxy or Bedrock Access Gateway to use Bedrock natively from the plugin.
</success_criteria>

<output>
Create `.planning/phases/08.1-streaming-transport-fix-bedrock-provider/08.1-02-SUMMARY.md` when all tasks complete. Document:
- Final bundle size measurement (before/after); whether the 1.2 MB ceiling was bumped (Rule 3 deviation if so)
- @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock version pinned + the 9 transitive Node-import errors confirmed for @aws-sdk/credential-providers (rejected per Pitfall 5)
- Files modified count + new files created
- Manual UAT instructions (which 2 of 4 auth methods the user should exercise — defaults from RESEARCH §Open Question 5)
- Any deviations from PATTERNS.md analog (especially the eslint sentence-case allowlist extension if needed for 'AWS Bedrock')
</output>
